Shareholders and interested parties are encouraged to participate and gain valuable information about the company's position in the market.

news.summary: U-Store, a self-storage business established in 1975 by partners Harry S. Sellers, a former U.S. Navy officer, and Bernard Fensterwald Jr., a DC Attorney, is celebrating its 50th anniversary in 2025. The family-owned company, with a history of five decades, has reached this milestone with pride.

news.summary: A significant investment of $25 million has been made by Leviton in its Network Solutions Fuquay-Varina, N.C. fiber optic cable manufacturing plant to expand its size. Today, Leviton's Network Solutions Business Unit disclosed the completion of the expansion project, showcasing a doubling in size due to the substantial financial commitment. This move marks a notable development for the company, highlighting its dedication to enhancing its manufacturing capabilities and potentially increasing its production output in the fiber optic cable sector.
news.headline: Leviton's Fiber Factory in N.C. doubled in size after $25 million investment. Expansion project completed. BOTHELL, Wash.

The properties up for sale are The Courte at Citrus Heights in Citrus Heights, California, and The Landing on Dundee in Wheeling, Illinois. news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 10, 2025, an inventor from Byford, WA, Australia shared about a breakthrough innovation. The inventor emphasized the paramount importance of time in emergency response operations. Thus, the inventor created the QUICKCUT TAC CARRIER to address this need efficiently. This invention aims to provide swift access to a quick cut saw during prolonged and critical emergency tasks. The inventor's ingenuity from Australia showcases the global reach of innovative solutions.
news.headline: Inventor from Australia creates QUICKCUT TAC CARRIER to carry quick cut saw for emergency response jobs.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025 in Pittsburgh, two inventors from West Los Angeles, California presented a new plumbing fixture aimed at preventing sewage blockages. The NO CLOG DRAIN was created to alleviate the need for costly plumbing repairs. This innovative design is projected to significantly reduce the number of clogged drains and associated maintenance costs. The inventors hope that this product will revolutionize the plumbing industry and bring about lasting benefits for households.
news.headline: InventHelp inventors create NO CLOG DRAIN to prevent sewer blockages, based in West Los Angeles, California.

news.summary: Today, a leading organization that provides Service Dogs for Veterans suffering from PTSD, TBI, and military sexual trauma played a key role in shaping legislation. news.summary: Entrepreneur and philanthropist Parker H. Petit, known for his extensive experience in both entrepreneurship and as a public company CEO, highlighted the need for readiness in facing these challenges. The warning comes amidst a rising number of legal hurdles confronting executives, emphasizing the importance of proactive measures in dealing with such issues. The statement was released in Atlanta on April 10, 2025, underscoring the need for vigilance and preparedness in navigating the evolving legal landscape for public companies.
news.headline: Pete Petit issues warnings to business leaders in book on legal challenges ahead.

news.summary: Hexnode, the enterprise software division of Mitsogo Inc., has introduced Hexnode UEM MSP, a specialized solution created to provide Managed Service Providers (MSPs) with enhanced device management features for multi-tenant setups. news.summary: WASHINGTON, April 15 marks National Laundry Day, coinciding with Tax Day. The American Cleaning Institute reveals that 80% of Americans favor cleaning over doing taxes. Highlighting the significance of laundry, a recent survey by Wakefield Research indicates that 47% of participants prioritize laundry as a means to maintain health. Doing laundry not only aids in cleanliness but also plays a crucial role in reducing allergens, as per the survey findings.
news.headline: April 15 is not just Tax Day, but also National Laundry Day, with 80% preferring cleaning over taxes. 47% see laundry as crucial for staying healthy.

news.summary: Global trade tensions have sparked investor interest in alternative assets such as Bitcoin and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) due to concerns over the long-term stability of the financial system. Despite US President Donald Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on higher reciprocal tariffs, tariffs on Chinese goods were escalated to 125%. This tariff escalation is viewed as a significant turning point for global markets, revealing deeper fractures in the monetary system. Tokenized gold trading volume surged to a two-year high, surpassing $1 billion, while onchain RWAs reached an all-time high of $20 billion, with tokenized private credit accounting for $12.7 billion. Analysts suggest that RWAs could reach a $50 billion all-time high by 2025. While some believe the tariffs are a US bargaining tool and not a lasting policy shift, others see them as negotiations posturing. The recovery of global risk assets, including the crypto market, may be influenced by the tone of the negotiations. Nansen analysts predict a 70% chance of the crypto market bottoming by June 2025.
news.headline: Trump’s tariff escalation highlights global financial system rifts as trade tensions persist. Tokenized assets see increased interest. Tariffs on Chinese goods rise from 104% to 125%. Top tokenized gold trading volume hits $1B; onchain RWAs reach $20B. Bitcoin's lack of upside may boost RWAs to $50B by 2025. Tariffs viewed as US bargaining tactic, not long-term policy shift. Recovery of global risk assets, including crypto, hinges on tariff negotiations. The potential to bottom by June 2025 seen.

news.summary: Protagonist Therapeutics, Inc. revealed groundbreaking new findings on their pill designed to address moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in a recent announcement. The Phase 3 trial encompassed both adolescents and adults, marking a significant advancement in the development of the treatment. This milestone emerged as part of the partnership with Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ), under the ICONIC program featuring icotrokinra (JNJ-2113). Data showcased at the 2025 World Congress of Pediatric Dermatology (WCPD) Annual Meeting demonstrated that adolescents treated with daily icotrokinra exhibited notably improved rates of clear or nearly clear skin by Week 16 compared to those on a placebo regimen. Notably, no new safety concerns were reported in the study. For more detailed information, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Protagonist and Johnson & Johnson report positive results for psoriasis oral pill study, achieving higher clear skin rates in adolescents with no safety issues. Study conducted on pill icotrokinra (JNJ-2113) in Phase 3 trial.
